---
title: "New essay: what happens at high energy?"
description: >-
  A new essay answers the standard high-energy objection to discrete
  spacetime — on the A=1 lattice, inertia and amplitude are one
  conserved quantity split by the phase-rate, and that same rate caps
  energy per mode.
author: "Jack D. Menendez"
date: "2026-06-03"
categories: [essay, dispersion, high-energy, inertia]
---

A new essay,
[*What happens at high energy?*](../../essays/posts/2026-06-03-inertia-amplitude-and-high-energy/index.qmd),
is now on the [essays page](../../essays/index.qmd). It takes on the
standard objection to discrete spacetime — that a lattice must misbehave
at high energy, breaking Lorentz invariance or hiding an arbitrary cutoff
— and shows the answer is already in the bipartite Dirac update derived in
[Paper I](../../papers/paper-01-geometry-first.qmd), not bolted on.

The short version: there is only **one** amplitude per site, and the rate
at which its phase advances, $\Delta\phi=\omega+V$, decides how much of it
*propagates* versus *stays in place*. Staying in place is what inertia
**is** here — the fraction $\sin^2(\Delta\phi/2)$ the phase-rate diverts
from hopping — so mass and inertia are two readings of one quantity, not a
second substance. The same phase-rate read as momentum enters through a
structure factor that **saturates** at the zone boundary, so energy and
momentum per mode are bounded: the lattice is its own ultraviolet
regulator, for free, and the familiar $E^2=m^2+|\mathbf p|^2$ is the
*low-energy limit*. Nothing in the substrate bends — the adjacency graph
is fixed; it is the derived energy–momentum curve that bends, the same
distinction that lets the framework carry gravity without a curved metric.

This maps onto three **`PASS`** rows in Paper I's audit table: *Photon
dispersion* (group velocity vs. wavenumber, with zone-boundary
corrections; `exp_09`), *Inertial persistence* (`exp_01`), and the *Dirac
equation* continuum limit. The essay is paired with the
[birefringence essay](../../essays/posts/2026-05-20-birefringence-from-dirac/index.qmd):
that one is about the *direction* of the same propagator, this one about
its *magnitude* and what it does to energy.

Pointers:

- Read the essay: [What happens at high energy?](../../essays/posts/2026-06-03-inertia-amplitude-and-high-energy/index.qmd)
- Companion essay: [Birefringence in the A=1 lattice](../../essays/posts/2026-05-20-birefringence-from-dirac/index.qmd)
- Substrate paper: [Paper I — Geometry First](../../papers/paper-01-geometry-first.qmd)
- All essays: [essays page](../../essays/index.qmd)
